IBEW.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Indoor Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-481 Indianapolis, IN** is running the hottest board right now — 43 calls across 191 hands with **$54.75 scale** and real money stacked on top. Meade Electric's Meta Data Center gig pays **10% over scale ($59.13/hr)** on 5-12's Mon-Fri plus Saturday work; Ermco's Google Data Center in Mooresville is solid inside new construction, 5-10's plus Saturday. Both contractors are burning through bodies on legit long-term work.

**LU-430 Racine, WI** is the surest bet for a quick book-clear: 11 calls, only 76 hands, **$62.51 scale** flat. Four different contractors (Gerdes, Pieper, Staff Electric, Kelso Burnett) all feeding the Microsoft Mount Pleasant project with identical wages and long-sleeve requirements. If you've got reciprocal or WI endorsement, you're on the job in days, not weeks.

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is chasing you hard — Parsons Electric's Polaris Forge Data Center is dangling **$44.80/hr plus $100/day per diem (non-taxed)** for six-tens, with **$100 weekday and $150 weekend incentives**. The GPS radius trigger (100+ miles) makes the money land tax-free if you're traveling in. Hunt Electric and Apex have slots too, including line work at **$58.50/hr**.

**LU-26 Washington, DC** has 38 calls and **$125/day per diem** floating on FIS, LLC's VA Data Center jobs in Sterling — active data center with planned overtime. JE Richards and Valid Electric are moving people through Manassas and Sterling on 5-8 schedules with 2 OT hours daily.

**LU-613 Atlanta, GA** runs wide and steady — 39 calls, top **$48.00 scale**, with Inglett & Stubbs feeding QTS Fayette and Wellstar Hospital on 4-10's, and Miller Electrical running splicer interviews at **$40.90/hr** for cable work.

Data center work is eating the board: 9 of the top 10 locals are chasing the same infrastructure builds across the Belt and Mountain West.

LINEMAN.text:
**Trampin the IBEW Lineman Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-1426 Grand Forks, ND** is running hot with 25 calls and only ~143 hands covering them — that's tight. Parsons Electric has stacked 84 Inside JW slots across three separate calls on the Polaris Forge Data Center in Harwood, all **$44.80/hr** with **$100/day non-taxed per diem** plus **$100–$150 daily incentive** (double on weekends) running 6-10s. Hunt Electric's 10-slot Air Base call pays the same scale but with straight 4-10s and Friday OT. Data center work is moving the needle here.

**LU-71 Columbus, OH** has 34 calls chasing 109 hands — serious board depth. NEW RIVER ELECTRIC dominates with 47 Journeyman Lineman slots: 37 in Columbus (distro, **$125/night per diem** plus **$100 bonus**) and 10 in Southern Ohio, both requiring CDL A. JJM POWER's 6-hand Chillicothe crew (South Central distro, 4-10s, **$100/day per diem**) and Thayer's 4-hand Columbus make-ready call (hotels paid, **$500–$600 bonus**) round out solid transmission and distribution rebuild work at or near top scale **$61.44/hr**.

**LU-583 El Paso, TX** shows 10 calls needing 114 hands — book's strained. Summit Line, Cupertino Electric, and B&D Industries each post Inside JW at **$55/hr** (all OT double), though Rosendin underbids at **$40 + $15 incentive**. Catch: Summit and Cupertino require NM State JIW License EE98J; B&D wants fire alarm/VESDA experience. Projects stay unnamed but rates hold steady.

**LU-1393 Indianapolis, IN** has 24 calls on 36 hands — book's moving. Newkirk Electric's Muncie T-line work and ARC American's 34kv/12.5kv rebuilds near Wanatah span **$51.79–$64.66/hr** with per diems up to **$150/day**, covering transmission and substation work across the jurisdiction.

**LU-342 Greensboro, NC** has only 2 calls but Enovum's Data Center pulled 30 Inside JW at **double-time OT** on 6-10s with **$10/day oversale per diem** — that's aggressive overhead for a non-union rate market.

Data centers and transmission rebuilds are pulling hands nationwide; 1,130 workers chasing 362 calls means the board's still buyer-favoring outside contractors.

UA.text:
**Trampin the UA Locals — Monday, July 13, 2026**

**LU-290 Portland, OR** is the board to watch this week — 24 calls burning across commercial and residential HVAC, refrigeration service, and BIM detailing work. PermaCold and Dr. HVAC are running steady residential and commercial loops in the Portland-McMinnville corridor, while Apollo's pulling in detailers for Intel's Ronler Acres campus in Tigard with Revit and AutoCAD MEP skills. Book depth is tight at roughly 28 hands against the call count, meaning early dispatches if you can detail or cert on VRF systems.

**LU-208 Denver, CO** has 19 solid calls anchored by Kiewit's Fort St. Vrain Powerhouse rebuild — nights 5/9's (5pm–2am, 9.5 hours), **$65/day per diem**, and steady fitter work. Olson Plumbing's Parker Advent Hospital job runs cleaner 4/10 schedules. US Eng. Metalworks has night-shift fab work, and Murphy's running combo welders on 6/10 night shifts with shift differential. About 46 hands deep on this one, so it's moving.

**LU-213 St John, NB** is a refinery play — Irving Oil is the anchor, with Stuart Olson, Cam, and Lorneville all posting journeyman pipefitter calls under GPMA and NMA agreements. Day shift, short-term conditions, and 8 calls chasing 31 hands makes this a quick dispatch if you can cross the border.

**LU-525 Las Vegas, NV** keeps the Strip humming — Hansen Mech has Palazzo, Venetian, and Hard Rock plumber calls (27 hands open), while S3H is staffing Four Seasons journeymen. Light depth relative to calls, tight labor window.

**LU-192 Cheyenne, WY** — PMI's running Ellsworth AFB and Fort Meade VA Hospital jobs with **$100/day per diem**, OSHA 10 required, combo welders and plumbers. Minimal board (6 hands, 2 calls), but federal work pays steady.

Refinery and data-center infrastructure is pulling hands east and north; power generation and hospital builds are holding the mountain west tight.
